name: wp-plugin
summary: Bootstrap a brand-new PHPNomad WordPress plugin from a freshly created project directory.
description: Scaffolds the entire structure of a PHPNomad WordPress plugin: entry file, Application class, root initializer, composer.json, tests, configuration. Run once after `composer create-project phpnomad/wp-plugin-starter`.
problem: You want to start a new WordPress plugin built on PHPNomad and you do not want to clone a stale template, hand-edit placeholder names, or wire up DI bindings yourself. You want a working baseline you can extend with other recipes.
appliesWhen: You are creating a brand-new WordPress plugin (not adding to an existing one)
You ran `composer create-project phpnomad/wp-plugin-starter <name>` and are inside that directory
You want the canonical PHPNomad-on-WordPress plugin structure
You will use phpnomad/cli to add functionality after this recipe completes
avoidWhen: You already have a working PHPNomad plugin and only want to add functionality (use other recipes instead)
You are integrating PHPNomad into an existing non-PHPNomad WordPress plugin (this overwrites composer.json)
You want a multi-segment PHP namespace right out of the box (this v1 supports single-segment only; edit composer.json after for multi-segment)
examples: Start a new WordPress plugin called Acme Billing
I just ran composer create-project, set up the rest
Scaffold an empty WordPress plugin with PHPNomad
tradeoffs: Overwrites the starter's composer.json with one that reflects your plugin's identity. Run this only once, in a freshly created project. Multi-segment namespaces require a manual edit afterward.
outputs: {{textDomain}}.php — WordPress plugin entry file with proper plugin header
lib/Application.php — PHPNomad Application class wired for WordPress
lib/AppInit.php — root initializer where future recipes register
composer.json — rewritten with your plugin's identity, deps, and PSR-4 autoload
.phpnomad/config.json — recipe activation list, pre-seeded with the common recipes
postApply: Run `composer update` to pick up any new deps in the rewritten composer.json
Run `composer dump-autoload` so the new namespace mapping is active
Use `vendor/bin/phpnomad recipes:list` to see what other recipes are available
Add functionality with phpnomad/datastore, phpnomad/controller, phpnomad/listener, etc.
When ready, install in a WordPress site to verify the plugin activates

pluginName.description: Display name shown in the WordPress admin (e.g., 'My Plugin')
pluginName.aiHint: Title-case human-readable name. Shown in the WP plugins screen. Two to four words.

description.description: Short description for the WP plugin header and composer.json
description.example: Subscription billing for the Acme service.
description.aiHint: One sentence, ends with a period. Plain English, no marketing fluff.

vendor.aiHint: lowercase, may contain dashes, no spaces. Often the company or developer's handle.

package.aiHint: lowercase, dash-separated, no spaces. The plugin's slug.

namespace.description: Single-segment PHP namespace for the plugin's classes (PascalCase, no backslashes)
namespace.aiHint: Single segment, PascalCase, no backslashes. Combine vendor and package into one segment (AcmeBilling, MyPlugin). Multi-segment namespaces are not supported in v1 of this recipe.

textDomain.description: WordPress text domain for i18n (lowercase, dash-separated). Also becomes the entry filename.
textDomain.aiHint: lowercase, dash-separated. Convention is to match the package name.

authorName.description: Author name shown in the WP plugin header and composer.json

authorEmail.aiHint: Valid email address. Used in composer.json author block.

authorUrl.description: Author website URL for the WP plugin header (optional, leave empty for none)
authorUrl.aiHint: Optional. Pass an empty string if not applicable.
